title: Maybe You Should Talk to Someone
subtitle: A Therapist, Her Therapist, and Our Lives Revealed
author: Lori Gottlieb
themes: Therapists are human — they struggle with the same issues as their patients
Therapy is a collaborative process of self-discovery, not a passive treatment
Resistance is a normal part of change, not a sign of failure
Universal human struggles: loneliness, grief, meaning, mortality, connection
The therapeutic relationship is the vehicle for healing
excerpt: Therapists talk to people all day about the most intimate details of their lives, but somehow it's always the therapist who remains a mystery — the person behind the professional facade. People often say to me, 'You probably think I'm crazy.' But what I think is: You're not crazy. You're just human. We all are.
